entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 18 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-18 19:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • A pre-loved designer handbag for $500? It’s not too good to be true
  • ‘Too tired to keep track’: The struggle of writing a book with a newborn in tow
  • How a wild dolphin taught Melody Horrill to love and trust again
  • TV sweetheart and former nominee snubbed from Logies
  • How the cost of your Sunday roast went up almost 40% in three years
  • ‘Real risk’: Breast implant complications rise if doctor not a specialist surgeon
  • Mind the gap: Calling out movies pairing men with much younger women
  • Author Anne Tyler: ‘What am I going to do with my life? I’m only 80!’
  • ‘In it together’: How to tackle teacher burnout to avoid mass exodus
  • ‘Not like an essay for school’: Sydney teen published in prestigious medical journal
  • Xavier College under pressure to remove alleged abuser’s name from arts building
  • Pre-retirees told not to panic as market meltdown hits super balances
  • Lightscape festival to mesmerise at Botanic Gardens
  • City2Murphy: Sydney Queer Irish find their feet in harbour city
  • Patience and love: the crucial ingredients for childcare reform
  • The surprising offer from Sky News that I had to refuse
  • ‘Everyone knew our names, but not our faces’: when footy had an escape valve
  • Prime ministers, popes and princes: a day in the life of a protective service agent
  • Your complete EOFY checklist (can you tick every one?)
  • Melbourne’s much-loved community footy match is back, and with $100k to boot
  • Bipartisanship and progressive policy: is this what it feels like to be a Kiwi?
  • Federal government lobbying behind the scenes for Assange’s freedom
  • Anthony Albanese sets out domestic agenda for first sitting of new parliament
  • Best of cartoons, June 19, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Best of cartoons, June 18, 2022
  • COVID-19 jabs for under-fives ‘a couple of months’ away: Cheng
  • Performance pay, revamped school hours: Premier flags education reforms
  • Aboriginal flag to fly permanently atop Harbour Bridge by end of year
  • Libs deputy’s failures Ley it on the line
  • Youth homelessness can be overlooked but there is a way out – as Finn’s story proves
  • ‘A very dangerous operation’: Substation engulfed in flames south of Sydney
  • ‘Blight on our state’: NSW announces $100 million for women’s safety
  • Biden falls off his bike near his Delaware beach home
  • Japan has the lowest COVID death rate in the OECD without mandates. Here’s why
  • Monkey shot, tiger roams streets as toll mounts for cartels’ exotic pets
  • Colbert show staffers arrested at US Capitol after hours
  • Residents in shock as 200 kilogram tiger wanders streets in Mexico
  • Millions of homes under water as floods hit India, Bangladesh
  • Diplomats danced to ‘I Will Survive’ while closing historic Geneva trade deal
  • ‘A simple protest that got out of hand’: Trump defends role in Capitol attack
  • Eurovision rejects Ukraine call to host contest in Kyiv, talks to BBC
  • COVID-19 shots for babies and toddlers approved by US medical regulator
  • Discarded rubbish found on Mars
  • British government orders extradition of Julian Assange
  • Human remains found in Amazon belong to British journalist, exam confirms
  • Russian economy to overcome ‘reckless’ sanctions, Putin says
  • Gradually then suddenly: how energy markets failed and what happens next
  • Target Time and Superquiz, Sunday, June 19
  • Dozens of regional WA flights cancelled
  • Heroes, halos and home truths: students learn life skills the cool way
  • Fire tears through power station in Wollongong
  • Police investigate major fire at Mount Compass South Australia
  • Queensland’s strawberry prices soar
  • Family of hit and run victim urge driver to come forward
  • ‘I bring my sister a bag of lettuce’: The gardeners avoiding outrageous prices
  • Warren Buffett’s final charity lunch draws record $27 million winning bid
  • SpaceX reportedly fired employees behind open letter rebuking Elon Musk
  • The 25 best Australian novels of the last 25 years
  • Why Yassmin Abdel-Magied is a big proponent of the marriage contract
  • ‘I’m not afraid to fail’: Jim Courier on new challenges and hostile crowds
  • How Bluey went from a kids’ TV show to a global merchandising machine
  • ‘Run by dinosaurs’: the breath of fresh air women are injecting into Australian music
  • ‘It shocks me’: Melissa Leong on Australian TV and her chance at making history
  • Photorealistic AI images have arrived. Are artists in trouble?
  • National weather forecast for Saturday June 18
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: NY Times: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Gun Sellers Stoke Fears to Boost Weapon Sales
  • Uvalde Officer Passed Up Shot at Gunman for Fear of Hitting Children
  • Odesa Opera House Reopens Amid Putin’s War
  • Ukraine’s Death Workers: ‘If You Take It All Close to Heart, You Go Mad’
  • Zelensky Visits War-Torn Mykolaiv in Southern Ukraine
  • French Nuclear Power Crisis Frustrates Europe’s Push to Quit Russian Energy
  • Jan. 6 Hearings Underscore Hard Truths About Democracy
  • Amid Jan. 6 Revelations, Election Lies Still Dominate the G.O.P.

  • How Many N.Y. Democrats Does It Take to Fill a House Seat? Try 15.

  • A Vaccine for Young Children? U.S. Parents Have Mixed Reactions
  • Juneteenth Is a U.S. Holiday, but Not a Day Off In Most States

  • Infighting Overshadows Big Plans at The Washington Post
  • Ex-Amazon Worker Convicted in Capital One Hacking
  • ‘The Dirty Secret of Covid’: Scott Galloway on the Postpandemic Economic Turmoil
  • Help! It’s Been More Than 2 Years and I Still Don’t Have the Refund
  • Huge Fire Breaks Out at Sinopec’s Shanghai Complex, Leaving 1 Dead

entry.title: Guardian: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Zelenskiy visits frontline troops in Mykolaiv; UK with Ukraine ‘for the long haul’, says Johnson – live
  • US Open golf 2022: third round – live!
  • £68m of cocaine delivered with bananas to two supermarkets in Czech Republic
  • Sir Ringo Starr among those wishing Sir Paul McCartney a happy 80th birthday
  • Brazil police arrest third suspect in killings of Dom Phillips and Bruno Pereira
  • Group of British MPs and peers say Parthenon marbles must return to Greece

  • Gunman, 70, kills three people at Alabama church dinner
  • Revealed: why Van Gogh’s ‘empty chair’ paintings were never shown together

  • Summer holidays: is it cheaper to go to the UK, France or Spain?
  • ‘I call it sauna head – your ego disappears’: how the UK got hooked on heat

  • Yotam Ottolenghi’s recipes for no-cook cooking
  • The book that tore publishing apart: ‘Harm has been done, and now everyone’s afraid’
  • The pandemic has taken its toll on our friendships. How do we fix them? | Claire Cohen

  • At least 25 dead and millions stranded as floods devastate India and Bangladesh

  • Destination masterpiece: 75 great artworks to see across the UK

  • ‘There was practically a riot at King’s Cross’: an oral history of Harry Potter at 25
  • Heat wave: how Orkney is leading a tidal power revolution
  • Ukraine’s first lady Olena Zelenska on being Russia’s target No 2: ‘When you see their crimes, maybe they really are capable of anything’

  • Blind date: ‘His eyes lit up when we started talking about potatoes’
  • Everyone relax: K-pop superfans say BTS hiatus is NBD
  • Tim Dowling: my dad is 100 but I’m the one with ‘old person’s problems’
  • Smoke clouds Shanghai as chemical plant fire leaves at least one dead

  • ‘All the worst things you can think of’: Christchurch residents stricken by city’s stench

entry.title: Washington Post: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 18 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-18 17:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Masks, Covid-19 test kits, plastic bottles: more than 9,000kg of litter found on Hong Kong’s hiking trails, seashore last year, green group says
  • Fraudsters swindled HK$1.28 billion from more than 6,000 Hongkongers through online, phone scams in first 4 months of year
  • Hong Kong’s buyers snap up 70 per cent of new homes on offer, casting aside HKMA’s 75-point interest hike as banks deferred raising mortgage rates
  • Hong Kong must prepare for uncertainty as US-China tensions will continue, but opportunities are still present as country welcomes American investors: city’s finance chief
  • Greater Bay Area: can China ‘integrate’ Hong Kong into its southern economic powerhouse?
  • Coronavirus: business at Hong Kong bars, pubs falls 60 per cent amid new Covid rapid-test rule, industry leaders say, as city logs 1,160 local cases
  • Coronavirus: 150 Hong Kong passengers in logistics nightmare after Amsterdam airport staff shortfall, tough quarantine rules in city leave them ‘scared, helpless and clueless’
  • Hey, Hong Kong – how about we stop being racist and include foreign domestic workers in the consumption voucher scheme?
  • HKEX considers opening offices in New York and London as it takes the battle for IPO fundraising to North America and Europe
  • Surviving Sars (2002-2007): Hong Kong’s first major health and political hurdles in 25 photos
  • ‘I need a room’: overseas Hong Kong students struggling to book quarantine hotels for summer return to city
  • Hong Kong vs Shenzhen: a technology tale of 2 cities, and a game of catch-up
  • Biden, China’s Xi ‘will talk’ says US president, weighing action on tariffs
  • Beijing is likely to step up its campaign to ‘reunify’ with Taiwan, analysts say
  • China-US rivalry dampens belt and road investment in Latin America and the Caribbean, but strong demand remains
  • Chinese disciplinary watchdog to investigate after bank protesters flagged as health risk
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping warns against international decoupling in Russia speech
  • Biden urges China, other major economies to step up climate action, as US and EU join global methane cuts pledge
  • China to tighten grip on social media comments, requiring sites to employ sufficient content moderators
  • China’s latest hot export is a US$50 toy Russian fighter jet that ‘will never break’, helped by social media such as TikTok
  • Huge blaze at Shanghai Sinopec plant kills one
  • Chinese university student wins Apple’s Swift coding challenge with genetics experiment simulation
  • Coronavirus: which works better to handle a pandemic – democracy or autocracy?
  • Tencent’s WeChat debuts in 618 shopping festival with big bet on live-streaming e-commerce despite slowing economy
  • China’s US$1.5 trillion opportunity: private pension scheme entices Fidelity, Manulife, Invesco, Hong Kong asset managers
  • China manufacturing: fresh lay-offs in the Pearl River Delta add to worries over economic slowdown
  • BTS: US$3.5 million Proof album purchase shows V’s fans in China are undeterred by Beijing’s K-Culture crackdown
  • Elon Musk envisions remaking Twitter in WeChat’s image
  • Former Japanese politician’s appeal against drug trafficking conviction to be heard in Chinese court
  • TikTok moves US user data to Oracle servers amid concerns over China
  • How China inadvertently became the dominant power in Central Asia – and is radically reshaping the region
  • New US Senate bill proposes expansion of Taiwan military aid
  • Singapore’s Pink Dot gay pride rally returns, as MP from ruling party attends for first time – and in a pink T-shirt
  • ‘Never seen such floods in her life’: 41 dead, millions stranded in India, Bangladesh
  • The way we wed: how Indian brides are reinventing ceremonies to push for gender equality
  • Oil-producing Gulf nations are pursuing greener energy. What does this mean for Asia?
  • US, Marshall Islands to speed up economic assistance deal to counter China’s Pacific influence
  • As Japan court ruling nears, LGBT groups hope for new milestone in fight for equality
  • China’s Covid-19 health code system is ripe for abuse and must not outlast the pandemic
  • Why Singapore may be edging closer to scrapping Section 377A criminalising gay sex – and what would happen if it did
  • Kate Bush classic Running Up That Hill tops UK chart after 37 years
  • Gunman, 70, kills 3 senior citizens during potluck dinner at Alabama church
  • US billionaire Warren Buffett’s last charity private lunch sees record US$19 million bid
  • Russia frees Ukraine medic who filmed Mariupol horror, saved both sides’ people
  • Boris sings out for Ukraine: UK PM Johnson says nation deserves to host Eurovision
  • UK begins electronically tagging migrants coming via ‘unnecessary, dangerous routes’
  • Woman arrested for making anti-Asian remarks, pepper spraying 4 people in New York
  • Ukraine war: captured US fighters Alexander Drueke and Andy Huynh surface in Russian state TV videos
  • Biden says meeting with crown prince not focus of Saudi Arabia trip amid criticism
  • SpaceX fires workers behind letter criticising Elon Musk’s behaviour
  • Google co-founder Sergey Brin seeks divorce from second wife Nicole Shanahan after 3 years of marriage
  • Emmanuel Macron’s awkward moment with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelensky becomes internet meme
  • Post-lockdown Shanghai among top spending cities during China’s 618 online shopping extravaganza
  • ByteDance closes Shanghai games studio in retreat from industry dominated by Tencent, NetEase
  • LME to require weekly OTC position reporting as it seeks to address nickel chaos
  • Sweet Home on Netflix: seasons 2 and 3, new stars for monster K-drama series
  • ‘He shared with me his experience with stress – that was really helpful’: why men should face mental health issues head on and talk about them
  • Why sommeliers hate it when you BYOB – a Hong Kong insider explains why
  • Taylor Swift then and now: 8 things you might not know about the singer, from her early financial adviser dreams to why she feels like an outsider
  • Traditional artisans front and centre of tourism promotion in Hanoi as Vietnamese capital seeks to preserve dying craftsman communities
  • Zero-waste bulk food and eco-friendly product retailer Live Zero in Hong Kong to open new flagship store, part of founder’s fresh strategy
  • The Chinatown Stalin made a ghost town: Millionka in Vladivostok, home for labourers who built the Russian Far East
  • The Zendaya of Indonesia, Zsazsa Utari – model, actress and dancer – looks to shake up its entertainment industry
  • Who are the richest Fast & Furious stars? Net worths, ranked – from Dwayne ‘The Rock’ Johnson and Vin Diesel, to Wonder Woman’s Gal Gadot and the late Paul Walker, here’s who raked in millions
  • Meet Elon Musk’s sister Tosca, founder of Netflix-style streaming service Passionflix, accomplished filmmaker and mother of twins – but did her brother invest in her company?
  • 13 little-known facts about Lightyear star Chris Evans, from crushing on Sandra Bullock and matching Avengers tattoos with Scarlett Johansson, to starring in a Marilyn Manson music video
  • The luxury life of Formula One’s George Russell: how the Mercedes driver and star of Netflix’s Formula 1: Drive to Survive spends big on luxury cars, designer suits and holidays with his girlfriend
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Bitcoin slumps below key $20,000 threshold
  • Bitcoin mining: surging production costs crimp crypto margins

  • Let’s not talk about sex

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
